channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How an ancient resin traded for centuries got snarled up by the Iran war
item.description: It's not just energy supplies that have been disrupted by the Iran war. It's also hitting frankincense, a commodity that's been defining trade routes in the Middle East for thousands of years.
item.content:encoded:
It's not just energy supplies that have been disrupted by the Iran war. It's also hitting frankincense, a commodity that's been defining trade routes in the Middle East for thousands of years.

(Image credit: Maha Loubaris)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: How a cheese sandwich at the Masters is still $1.50 in an era of price hikes
item.description: The priciest concessions at the Masters, beer and wine, cost just $6 each. The Georgia golf tournament prides itself on a simple and affordable menu, even as ticket prices continue to climb.
item.content:encoded:
The priciest concessions at the Masters, beer and wine, cost just $6 each. The Georgia golf tournament prides itself on a simple and affordable menu, even as ticket prices continue to climb.

(Image credit: Andrew Redington)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Financial markets welcome Iran cease-fire but caution remains
item.description: Stocks rallied Wednesday on news of a two-week cease fire in the U.S. war with Iran. It could take some time, though, before drivers feel relief at the gas pump.
item.content:encoded: Stocks rallied Wednesday on news of a two-week cease fire in the U.S. war with Iran. It could take some time, though, before drivers feel relief at the gas pump.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Oil prices plunge and stocks soar after U.S. and Iran agree on a ceasefire
item.description: Investors around the world breathed a sigh of relief at the prospect of peace — and an easing of the global energy crisis.
item.content:encoded:
Investors around the world breathed a sigh of relief at the prospect of peace — and an easing of the global energy crisis.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: Trump and the war in Iran give investors whiplash
item.description: The latest twist in what's already been a turbulent spring for Wall Street and global markets.
item.content:encoded: The latest twist in what's already been a turbulent spring for Wall Street and global markets.
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: A year after 'Liberation Day,' Trump's tariffs are hurting small businesses
item.description: Many small-business owners say the uncertainty that goes along with the high cost of the Trump administration's emergency tariffs is driving away customers and eating into profits.
item.content:encoded:
Many small-business owners say the uncertainty that goes along with the high cost of the Trump administration's emergency tariffs is driving away customers and eating into profits.

(Image credit: Andrew Schneider)
item.dc:creator: Andrew Schneider

item.title: How does the economy shape our lives? Planet Money has a book for that!
item.description: NPR's Planet Money is publishing its first book! The team's newest project aims to help audiences navigate the economy and their lives.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Planet Money is publishing its first book! The team's newest project aims to help audiences navigate the economy and their lives.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Gonzalez

item.title: Beer cans, helium balloons and mortgages: An unexpected mix of things affected by war
item.description: It's not just oil and gas that are affected by the Iran war. All sorts of shortages and price spikes are starting to pop up that stand to affect people's daily lives.
item.content:encoded:
It's not just oil and gas that are affected by the Iran war. All sorts of shortages and price spikes are starting to pop up that stand to affect people's daily lives.

(Image credit: Brent Jones)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: An analysis of new U.S. employment growth data
item.description: New data from the Labor Department shows stronger than expected jobs growth. NPR's Scott Simon asks Heather Long, chief economist at Navy Federal Credit Union, for her reaction.
item.content:encoded: New data from the Labor Department shows stronger than expected jobs growth. NPR's Scott Simon asks Heather Long, chief economist at Navy Federal Credit Union, for her reaction.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: When legal sports betting surges, so do Americans' financial problems
item.description: As online betting has grown in popularity, a new report from the New York Federal Reserve builds on the troubling link between legal sports wagering and financial health.
item.content:encoded:
As online betting has grown in popularity, a new report from the New York Federal Reserve builds on the troubling link between legal sports wagering and financial health.

(Image credit: Charlie Riedel)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise
